behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: collectors -> aggregation payload -> summary step. A unit test of one function is too narrow because omission bugs appear when source adapters and aggregator are wired together. No real OS/network/subprocess is required for this specific behavior; deterministic fakes for the three sources are sufficient. Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified because behavior text only requires verifying payload composition at the internal handoff; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because it does not require judging model output.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/aggregation modules with three deterministic source stubs, execute one report build, and assert the exact payload sent into summary generation contains fields from metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering (source adapter failure -> report section note -> pipeline continues). Needs wiring of retrieval, error handling, and report assembly, so Unit is too low. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not require real network/OS conditions; an in-process failing source stub can reproduce 'unavailable'. It also does not depend on model quality decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two healthy source stubs and one stub that raises an unavailable error; assert process completes, output includes `data unavailable` in the correct section, and other sections are still populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model output quality (faithful representation of key values). A deterministic stub would always pass and cannot reveal hallucination/omission/distortion failures. Rejected higher level Workflow: behavior describes one model judgment step (summary generation), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation against the real model on representative inputs with known key values; assert factual consistency criteria (all required key values preserved, no contradictory values) across repeated runs with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external delivery/inbox behavior (real HTTP/API or SMTP path), which in-process fakes can miss (auth, transport, provider response semantics, inbox routing). Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior text targets end-to-end delivery to inbox, not evaluating model decision quality or multiple model-decision stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run scheduled script end-to-end in a test environment with real integrations (or production-like test tenants), then verify the configured recipient mailbox receives the email with expected report structure and metadata.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly spans external service outage plus persistence across runs. Real failure boundary includes infrastructure semantics (delivery endpoint unreachable), durable file persistence, and next-run retry behavior; pure mocks risk hiding contract/timing/state issues. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model-output-quality requirement and no multi-agentic decision chain is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real delivery failure in a test environment (e.g., blocked/unreachable mail endpoint), run the job and assert fallback file is written; execute the next scheduled run and verify previously saved report is retried and delivered when service is restored.
